sources.notes: Official Round 1082 tutorial checked as the main source.
Used to cross-check the first/last occurrence span and active-interval maintenance.

annotation.summary: After each point update, find the maximum length k for which two different length-k subarrays have equal multisets, and count triples attaining that maximum.
annotation.constraints: n and q are large, so each update must touch only the old and new values' occurrence sets and global summaries.
annotation.core_idea: For any value with first-last occurrence span d, adjacent subarrays between these endpoints have equal multisets, so k is at least d. Conversely, any two equal-multiset length-k subarrays imply some matched equal value spans distance at least k. Thus kmax is the maximum first-last span over values. The count is maintained by marking starts for values with span kmax; consecutive marked starts form components contributing c(c+1)/2 pairs.
annotation.complexity: O(log n) or O(log^2 n) per update depending on the interval-set implementation.
tricks: maximum answer equals maximum first-last occurrence span
count triples by components of consecutive active starts
remove and reinsert only changed values

solution_variants.name: dynamic-span-components
solution_variants.summary: Maintain each value's ordered occurrence set, the maximum span multiset, and component contributions of starts whose values attain each span.

tags.evidence: The solution maintains ordered occurrence sets per value and a global multiset of current first-last spans under updates.
The optimal k depends only on the first and last occurrence of each value, not on all equal pairs.
The number of optimal triples is maintained by updating component contributions of consecutive active starts.
The implementation has careful set updates, but implementation detail is not the main retrieval point.
